ground_truth.title: Master of Professional Accounting Degree | Walton College | University of Arkansas
ground_truth.main_content: The Master of Professional Accounting (MPA) program provides rigorous preparation at the graduate level for students to achieve success in their chosen career path in public practice, industry, or government. The MPA program offers three concentrations for targeted future professional career paths in accounting, including Assurance and Analytics, Taxation, and Corporate Accounting. These concentrations provide opportunity for professional internship credit, and can lead to qualification for the Certified Public Accounting exam. The program is one year in length with new cohorts beginning each Fall or Spring semester. Admission is selective and enrollment is limited. Students must complete the GMAT entrance test, though a waiver may be available with a 3.2 undergraduate GPA or 3 years of applicable professional work experience. The total estimated cost is $21,512.70 for in-state tuition and fees. The application deadline is March 1. The MPA does not require a thesis or comprehensive exam. Students must complete 30 hours of coursework including core classes such as ACCT 51203: Corporate Governance and Professionalism, ACCT 54103: Advanced Financial Accounting, ACCT 55203: Advanced Accounting Information Systems, and a professional internship or graduate business elective. Prerequisites include Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Finite Math or Calculus, Basic Statistics, Business Law, Management, Management Information Systems, Corporate Finance, Financial Accounting, Managerial/Cost Accounting, Accounting Technology, Auditing, and Taxation. Students must maintain a 3.00 grade-point average on all graduate coursework and all accounting coursework. The Department of Accounting programs are accredited by the AACSB International.
with: The Master of Professional Accounting (MPA) program provides rigorous preparation at the graduate level for students to achieve success in their chosen career path in public practice, industry, or government.
The MPA program offers three concentrations for targeted future professional career paths in accounting, including Assurance and Analytics, Taxation, and Corporate Accounting.
The accounting department at the Walton College enjoys strong relationships with corporate partners through networking events, speaking events, and academic activities.
To obtain the Master of Professional Accounting degree, students must complete the following 30 hours of coursework.
Department of Accounting programs are accredited by the AACSB International – The Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business.

items.caption: Schedule a Time to Talk - Attend an information session, or schedule a time to speak with an enrollment advisor.
Apply for this Degree Program - Learn about requirements for this program and begin your admissions application.
AACSB Accreditation - Department of Accounting programs are accredited by the AACSB International – The Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business.
Grace Helmers, a Master of Professional Accounting student, has secured a postgraduate technical assistantship with the Financial Accounting Standards Board.
Accounting Workshop hosted by the accounting department featuring research findings from associate professors.
Cooper Gordy, Master of Professional Accounting '25: Ready for Work - 'I chose the MPAcc program because the accounting professors at the U of A are the best in the business.'
Walton Professor Receives Prestigious Faculty Gold Medal Award - Charles Leflar, clinical professor emeritus, was honored at the annual State and National Awards Reception.
Accounting Certificate Program Returns in April 2025 - The Office of Financial Affairs and Human Resources Talent Development Team present the 2025 Accounting Certificate Program.

Walton College Researchers Help Drive Accounting Standards Dialogue - Faculty members Kris D. Allee and Zac Wiebe attended an exclusive conference at the Financial Accounting Standards Board headquarters.
